breakdown.note: Curriculum is public, but materials and assessment require institutional login.
CMSP platform is robust and supports millions of concurrent users.
Complete lesson scripts — teacher receives exact content and projection materials.

São Paulo.shortSummary: Mandatory 'Tecnologia e Inovação' subject delivered via private platforms with fully scripted lessons. Materials and assessments are locked behind institutional login.
São Paulo.summary: A centralized platform-based model utilizing pre-defined scripts and pre-made slides through partnerships with private platforms. While focused on large-scale delivery, SEDUC restricts most pedagogical content to logged-in users, creating barriers to transparency and external collaboration.

details.label: Subject
details.text: Tecnologia e Inovação

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: 1 class-hour per week*

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: Mandatory for all students

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: Currículo Paulista — Educação Digital e Midiática (Aligned with CIEB standards)

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: 100% Implemented across the state public network.

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: Mandatory since 2020 — Inova Educação Program

breakdown.note: Curriculum and materials fully open; assessment results are only partially available.
Hosted on permanent Google Drive links — resilient and always accessible.
Detailed lesson guides and videos, though not all classes have editable slides.

Minas Gerais.shortSummary: Mandatory 'Digital Education' subject since 2026 with fully open, state-authored materials. Covers AI and Data Science, though assessment results are not yet public.
Minas Gerais.summary: Recently implemented computer science curriculum focused on digital literacy and Artificial Intelligence. The model prioritizes in-house audiovisual content through the 'Digital Training School' (Escola de Formação Digital), which is open to the public. However, practical implementation remains dependent on local infrastructure and assessment results are not yet publicly available.

details.label: Subject
details.text: Digital Education

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: 1 class (45 min) per week

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: Mandatory for all students

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: Referencial Curricular Computação na Educação Básica

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: 100% Implemented

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: Mandatory since 2026 (Resolution SEE nº 5.212/2025)

breakdown.note: Classroom materials are restricted; no public assessment guides available.
'Escola Tá ON' portal suffers frequent crashes and 404 errors.
Most technically deep framework of the four — covers AI and Data Science in detail.

Espírito Santo.shortSummary: Computational Thinking embedded transversally in Mathematics — no standalone subject. The curriculum is technically deep but the main resource platform suffers chronic instability.
Espírito Santo.summary: The state does not offer a standalone computing subject, integrating Computational Thinking as a transversal competency within the Mathematics curriculum. While the curricular framework is updated and broadly available for public consultation, access to practical teaching materials and lesson plans is hindered by the constant instability of the 'Escola Tá ON' platform.

details.label: Subject
details.text: Integrated into Mathematics

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: 0h (No standalone mandatory hours)

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: Transversal

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: Integrated into Mathematics and its Technologies Curriculum

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: 0% Implemented (as a standalone subject)

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: Not mandatory — integrated into Math since 2024

breakdown.note: CBTC and elective portfolio are public, but the 2025 Digital Education Curriculum is not yet formally published.
SED-SC portal is stable; New HS Google Sites well-maintained; curriculum PDFs consistently accessible.
Elective CCEs have structured pedagogical scripts. Mandatory transversal integration is still in early stages.

Santa Catarina.shortSummary: No mandatory standalone computing subject — Digital Culture and Computational Thinking exist as optional HS electives. From 2026, BNCC Computing is being implemented transversally, backed by R$ 80M in devices and 107 NTE coordinators.
Santa Catarina.summary: No mandatory standalone computing subject exists in the general curriculum — in High School, 'Digital Culture' and 'Computational Thinking' are optional elective curricular components chosen by students. From 2025, the state created a Digital Education Curriculum and launched the Estudantes Conectados program, investing R$ 80 million in chromebooks, interactive screens, and Maker labs across the network. BNCC Computação is being implemented transversally from 2026, supported by 107 Educational Technology Center coordinators.

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: HS: Optional Elective | MS: Transversal (from 2026)

details.label: Subject
details.text: HS: Cultura Digital / Pensamento Computacional (CCEs) | MS: BNCC Computing transversal

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: HS: Variable (student's elective choice) | MS: 0h standalone

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: CBTC EF (2019) + EM (2021) + Digital Education Curriculum (2025)

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: HS: CCEs active since 2022 | MS: Transversal rollout from 2026

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: CBTC EM since 2021 — BNCC Computing mandatory from 2026

breakdown.note: DCE-PA is public, but the 2025 Computing Curriculum Reference is not yet open. Pedagogical materials restricted to teachers.
SEDUC-PA portal is stable. DCE-PA accessible via PDF. SisPAE and Ciseb links work adequately.
Computing Reference maps BNCC axes to DCE-PA; Ciseb offers six detailed tracks. Implementation still in early cascade phase for regional schools.

Pará.shortSummary: No standalone computing subject — BNCC Computing implemented transversally from 2026, supported by the Ciseb Innovation Center in Belém. Open teaching materials are not yet publicly available.
Pará.summary: No standalone computing subject exists; the state is implementing BNCC Computação transversally from 2026 across all knowledge areas. A Computing Curriculum Reference was developed in 2025 with Fundação Telefônica Vivo, and the Ciseb (Innovation Center) in Belém offers hands-on immersions in robotics, AI, and digital fabrication for the entire network. The cascade training strategy — from Ciseb and Cefor to regional DRE trainers — is the main implementation vehicle, but open public teaching materials are not yet available.

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: Transversal

details.label: Subject
details.text: Integrated across all disciplines + Ciseb immersions (scheduled)

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: 0h standalone — Ciseb immersions optional

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: DCE-PA EF (2019) + EM (2021) + Computing Reference (2025)

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: In progress — trainer cascade underway across DREs in 2026

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: DCE-PA EM since 2021 — BNCC Computing mandatory from 2026

breakdown.note: Base curriculum (DRC-MT) is public, but workbooks, assessments, and the Plurall platform are closed to external audiences.
SEDUC-MT portal is stable and well-maintained. DRC-MT links via Google Sites work consistently.
No standalone computing section in the DRC-MT. Skills are diluted across BNCC digital culture competencies with no year-by-year mapping.

Mato Grosso.shortSummary: No standalone computing subject — digital competencies integrated transversally via robotics and the annual STEAM Science Fair. Pedagogical materials are delivered through a closed FGV ecosystem.
Mato Grosso.summary: No standalone computing subject, integrating digital competencies transversally through educational robotics (SIMROBÓTICA in over 200 schools) and its annual STEAM Science Fair. Pedagogical content is delivered through the Structured Teaching System (bimonthly workbooks and Plurall platform via FGV), whose materials operate within a closed ecosystem.

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: Transversal

details.label: Subject
details.text: Integrated into Math/Science + Robotics (enabled schools)

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: 0h standalone

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: DRC-MT + BNCC Computing

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: Transversal in progress | Robotics in ~204/628 schools

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: DRC-MT EM since 2021 | BNCC Computing mandatory from 2026

breakdown.note: Curriculum and skills are public, but pedagogical materials are scattered PDFs with no unified portal.
Main portal is stable, but pedagogical material PDFs frequently return server errors.
HS skills are mapped with EMIF codes; Digital Culture Guide has real depth for MS. No standalone CS skills in MS.

Pernambuco.shortSummary: One of the few states with a mandatory 'Tecnologia e Inovação' unit in High School covering CT, programming, and digital ethics. In Middle School, computing is transversally integrated via a 2025 Digital Culture Guide.
Pernambuco.summary: Pernambuco is one of the few states with a dedicated technology curricular unit in High School — 'Tecnologia e Inovação' — mandatory within the Formative Itineraries block for all students. In Middle School, computing is transversally integrated into Mathematics and Languages, supported by a Digital Culture Guide published in 2025. Pedagogical materials have real depth, but are distributed as scattered PDFs on the SEE portal, with no unified access platform.

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: HS: Mandatory | MS: Transversal

details.label: Subject
details.text: HS: Tecnologia e Inovação | MS: Cultura Digital (Math & Languages)

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: HS: 45–120h/year | MS: 0h standalone

details.text: Currículo de Pernambuco EM (rev. 2025) + Educação Digital e Midiática (2025–2026)

details.text: HS: ~100% implemented | MS: Transversal phase

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: HS: Mandatory since 2022 | MS: BNCC Computing from 2026

breakdown.note: High-quality curriculum and matrices are public, but pedagogical guides (Stations) are fragmented or require login.
Official PDFs are stable, but the Anísio Teixeira and 'Escola Tá ON' platforms face recurring downtime.
Deep pedagogical structure in the Knowledge Stations model, with transdisciplinary milestones and AI integration.

Bahia.shortSummary: Transdisciplinary 'Knowledge Stations' model with a Computational Thinking component focused on local problem-solving and AI. Access is enabled via the Conectar project and the Google ecosystem.
Bahia.summary: Bahia adopts the 'Knowledge Stations' model in High School, featuring a 'Computational Thinking' component focused on experimental praxis and territorial problem-solving. The state strategy emphasizes digital sovereignty and the use of AI, integrating technologies transdisciplinarily and utilizing the Conectar project to enable access via the Google ecosystem and mobile devices.

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: HS: Mandatory Itinerary | MS: Transversal

details.label: Subject
details.text: HS: Knowledge Stations IV – Computational Thinking | MS: Body Culture and Technologies

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: HS: 3 classes/week (120h/year) | MS: 0h standalone

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: Documento Referencial Curricular da Bahia (DCRB)

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: HS: 100% implemented | MS: Expansion phase

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: Mandatory since 2022 — New High School

breakdown.note: RCA (EF and EM) is publicly accessible but fragmented. No standalone computing curriculum published. Saber Mais and Cemeam are partially public with no specific computing content.

SEDUC-AM portal has irregular maintenance; old portal still hosts content. Saber Mais renders poorly. For rural and river-access schools, online access is often impossible — the state maintains DVD distribution as an offline strategy.
No public mapping of computing skills by grade, track or component. BNCC Computação has not yet been incorporated into a specific state curriculum document.

Amazonas.shortSummary: No standalone computing subject. BNCC Computação is still being incorporated. Main structural barrier is connectivity — only 64.2% of schools have internet access. Isolated initiatives via Saber Mais and Cemeam.
Amazonas.summary: No standalone computing subject exists. BNCC Computação is still being incorporated into the state curriculum, with no complementary document published as of 2026. Existing initiatives — a programming language course for teachers (2024), the Saber Mais platform, and the Cemeam Digital After-School program — are isolated and non-systematic. The main structural barrier is connectivity: only 64.2% of public schools have internet access (vs. 93.1% national average), with rural and indigenous areas still largely offline.

details.label: Subject
details.text: No standalone subject — BNCC Computação in transversal incorporation

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: 0h (standalone mandatory)

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: Transversal (no formal definition published)

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: Referencial Curricular Amazonense — EF (2020) + EM (2021) + BNCC Computação (mandatory from 2026, no state complement published)

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: BNCC Computação in curricular adaptation — no approved state document as of April 2026

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: RCA EM approved 2021; BNCC Computação mandatory from 2026 (no specific state act published)

breakdown.note: DCT-TO (EF and EM) is public and accessible. Elective portfolio is available. However, no BNCC Computação complement has been published, and robotics elective materials are for trained teachers only.
SEDUC-TO portal is stable and well-maintained. DCT and New High School links are functional. SAETO results published in the Official Gazette.
Robotics electives have pedagogical depth in participating schools. Computational thinking present in teacher training since 2017, but no public skills mapping by grade aligned to BNCC Computação.

Tocantins.shortSummary: Robotics electives across all 238 high schools since Aug/2025, backed by 28k Chromebooks and 2,380 kits via Profe Digital. BNCC Computação being incorporated transversally without a state-specific published document.
Tocantins.summary: No standalone computing subject, but Tocantins is one of the most advanced states in the North region for real digital infrastructure and pedagogy. Through Profe Digital, the state distributed 28,000 Chromebooks to students, 14,000 notebooks to teachers, and 2,380 robotics kits to 238 high schools (2025), with weekly robotics electives. Computational thinking and gamification have been part of teacher training since 2017. BNCC Computação is being incorporated transversally, with no state-specific complementary document published as of 2026.

details.label: Subject
details.text: HS: Robótica Eletiva + Programação Web (pioneer schools) | MS: BNCC Computação Transversal

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: HS: ~2h/week (per semester) | MS: 0h standalone

details.text: HS: Elective (Itinerary) + Transversal | MS: Transversal

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: Documento Curricular do Território do Tocantins (DCT-TO) — EF (2019) + EM (Res. CEE/TO nº 169/2022) + BNCC Computação (from 2026)

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: HS: Robotics electives in 238 schools since Aug/2025 | MS: BNCC Computação in transversal incorporation

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: DCT-TO EM 2022; Robotics electives Aug/2025; Computational thinking in teacher training since 2017

breakdown.note: DC-GO (EF, EM, and Ampliado) is public and well-documented. Elective catalog with 'Technology & Innovation' is public. Goiânia published a detailed BNCC Computação implementation guide as state model. Only gap: no official SEDUC-GO state computing supplement published.
SEDUC-GO portal is well-maintained. DC-GO and elective catalog links are functional. NetEscola operational. SAEGO results via CAEd accessible.

Elective catalog structures 'Technology & Innovation' with BNCC Computação axes. DC-GO Ampliado includes computing. Active educational robotics in 173+ schools with R$ 58M invested since 2022. No formal state document with skills mapped by grade.

Goiás.shortSummary: Tech & Innovation elective (BNCC-aligned) available in all HS, plus robotics in 173+ schools. 100% connectivity across 1,014 state schools and 135k+ Chromebooks distributed since 2021.
Goiás.summary: No standalone computing subject, but Goiás has one of the strongest real infrastructure advances: 100% of 1,014 state schools connected (target met in 2025), 135,000+ Chromebooks distributed since 2021, and R$ 58 million invested in educational robotics since 2022. Computing and technology are addressed through the 'Technology and Innovation' elective in High School, explicitly grounded in the three BNCC Computação axes, and through robotics in full-time schools and military colleges. BNCC Computação is being implemented transversally, with a detailed implementation guide published by Goiânia city as a model.

details.label: Subject
details.text: HS: Tecnologia e Inovação (Eletiva) + Robótica nos Cepis | MS: BNCC Computação Transversal + Robótica nos CEPMGs

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: HS: Variable/semester (elective) | MS: up to 4h/week (Cepis/CEPMGs)

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: HS: Elective (Itinerary) + Transversal | MS: Transversal + Robotics in full-time/military schools

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: DC-GO (EF 2018/Ampliado) + DC-GOEM (2022) + Catálogo de Eletivas (BNCC Computação) + BNCC Computação from 2026

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: HS: Tech & Innovation elective in all schools; Robotics in 173+ units | MS: BNCC Computação in transversal incorporation

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: DC-GO EF (2018); DC-GOEM approved 2022; Robotics since 2022; BNCC Computação mandatory 2026

breakdown.note: RCG (EF and EM) and Res. CEEd 379/2024 are public and detailed. RCG Computação was submitted to public consultation. Only gap: practical pedagogical materials (teaching sequences) still incipient publicly.
SEDUC-RS portal well-organized and stable. The 2024 flood disaster affected 700+ schools, disrupting infrastructure and pedagogical processes with effects still felt in 2025.

Res. CEEd 379/2024 is the most detailed mandatory computing regulation in Brazil — specifies subject name, grade levels, teacher profile, transitional period. RCG Computação maps skills. Practical teaching materials and technology electives still in early implementation phase.

Rio Grande do Sul.shortSummary: The most specific mandatory computing regulation in Brazil: Res. CEEd 379/2024 mandates 'Fundamentos de Computação' in Grade 5, Grade 9, and all HS series. Implementation slowed by 2024 floods and shortage of licensed Computing teachers.
Rio Grande do Sul.summary: The state has the most advanced and specific regulation for computing as a standalone curricular component in Brazil: CEEd Resolution 379/2024 mandates 'Fundamentos de Computação' in the 5th and 9th years of elementary school and in all High School grades, with a four-year transitional period (2024–2028) for teacher qualification. The RCG Computação was developed by a working group including the Brazilian Computing Society and went through public consultation. Practical implementation is slowed by the 2024 flood disaster affecting 700+ schools and a shortage of licensed Computing teachers.

details.label: Subject
details.text: Fundamentos de Computação (mandatory in Grade 5, Grade 9, and all HS series)

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: Defined by each school in the curricular matrix (no minimum specified by Res. 379/2024)

details.label: Delivery Model
details.text: Grade 5 & 9: Standalone mandatory component | Early EF: Component, project, or transversal | HS: Mandatory integrated to FGB

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: Referencial Curricular Gaúcho (RCG-EF 2018 + EM 2021) + RCG Computação (2024) + Res. CEEd nº 379/2024 + Res. CEEd nº 384/2025

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: Transitional implementation since 2024 — 4-year period (2024–2028); any teacher with computing training may teach during transition

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: Res. CEEd nº 379/2024 (06/03/2024); EM: Res. 384/2025 incorporates BNCC Computação to FGB

breakdown.note: Itinerary materials are public, but no assessment data was found.
Government sites are generally stable, with occasional outages.
Only high-level competency lists — no practical classroom scripts provided.

Rio de Janeiro.shortSummary: Technology itineraries exist only in full-time schools — no universal computing subject for the general stream. Teaching materials are locked behind authenticated portals.
Rio de Janeiro.summary: The state does not offer a universal computing component in its general education stream, providing technology itineraries only within full-time integral schools. Furthermore, pedagogical guides and teaching materials are locked behind authenticated portals, preventing public access and external oversight.

details.text: Itinerary (depends on student's specialty choice)

details.text: New High School Technology Itinerary (SEEDUC-RJ)

details.text: ~25% Implemented (restricted to full-time and technical schools)

Rio de Janeiro.footnote: * No specific subject exists in the general network; computing is treated as transversal content. In the New High School's technology itinerary, it includes the subjects 'Technology, Innovation, and Environment' and 'STEAM Culture'.
